ab: a makiteng ku driya i, ullaya i Alapawan naniyam ruma. temakesi ku kana pallibak na takesian i, muasall mi kikadru i Balangaw. aw patrengtreng mi lla kikadru i tukay.
ch: 小時候我們家住在泰源，上學以後，就搬到了台東，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Alapawan. After I started school, we moved to Taitungn and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: a temakesi ku lla kana ta-siyē i, muka ku temakesia dratu kakuwayana dra Yuēn-cu-min. malladram ku lla kana Yuēn-cu-min-cu i Taiwan i, marekamenimeni aw dalaman tu kakuwayanan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: driyama, a kasedean dra kadenunan kandri na ami. kuyku aw i baelli na mainayan i, muruma mi i drekall kiyumaumall dra maidrangan. maranger mi malladram drata kakuwayanan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: makawalu lla tu ami kana maidrang. amawna, kamawan driya dra alabalabat an menau ta.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ku kiyumallay na maidrang dra, “a manamanay nu kinasaiguwan na ngaiyan?” salaw dalus an temarapuyuma. saigu driya temarapairang, temaraamis aw temaradripung.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的卑南語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、阿美話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in puyuma, as well as Hokkien, Pangcah and Japanese.

ab: peniya ku kiyumall i, mukalladram ku lla. a makiteng driya na maidrang i, meladaladam temaradripung. aw a Amis tu kataguwin. tu llang kikarun i, a Pairairang. driyama, sadru tu kisaiguwan na ngaiyan.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是阿美族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Pangcah, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: a madradrayar mi kana maidrang i, benabati dra sadru dra kallalladrman kana bulletrenganan dratu kakuwayanan dra paallupan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: alla, adri mi maruwa paseket mukalladram dratu nirarengay i, ullaya naniyam lu-ing kana so-ci. karuwa mi driya masamasall paseket killengallengaw.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: a makiteng ku driya i, adri ku malladram drata kakuwayanan. salaw ku migela rarengay dra “a Yuēn-cu-min ku.” kema.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: marekasadru ku kinalladraman i, pananaan ku lla a Piniyumayan kema ku kemiyanger. a salaw kirekamelli aw a paaillingan lla dra trau.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為卑南族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Piniyumayan is unique and something to be proud of.
